title: URL Encoder / Decoder
description: Encode text to percent-encoded URL format or decode URL-encoded strings. Handles Unicode and Japanese characters. Free online tool.
error: Invalid URL-encoded input. Please check for incomplete % sequences.

howToUse.label: Choose a mode
howToUse.description: Select Encode to convert text to URL-encoded format, or Decode to convert percent-encoded text back to readable text.

howToUse.label: Enter your text
howToUse.description: Type or paste the input. Japanese and other Unicode characters are encoded as UTF-8 bytes (e.g., %E3%81%82 for あ).

howToUse.label: View the result
howToUse.description: The encoded or decoded result appears instantly in the output area.

howToUse.label: Copy or swap
howToUse.description: Click Copy to save the result. Use Swap to move the output to input and flip the mode for round-trip testing.

faq.question: What is URL encoding (percent encoding)?
faq.answer: URL encoding converts characters that are not allowed or have special meaning in URLs into a % sign followed by two hexadecimal digits. For example, a space becomes %20, and あ becomes %E3%81%82 (its UTF-8 bytes in percent notation).

faq.question: What is the difference between encodeURIComponent and encodeURI?
faq.answer: encodeURIComponent (used by this tool) encodes almost all special characters, including /, ?, #, and &. encodeURI is less aggressive and is designed to encode a complete URI while leaving its structural characters intact.

faq.question: Is URL encoding the same as percent encoding?
faq.answer: Yes. URL encoding and percent encoding refer to the same mechanism defined in RFC 3986. The term 'percent encoding' is more formal; 'URL encoding' is widely used in everyday conversation.

faq.question: Does it handle Japanese and other Unicode text?
faq.answer: Yes. Non-ASCII characters including Japanese are first converted to UTF-8 bytes, then each byte is percent-encoded. For example, 日 is encoded as %E6%97%A5.
